steps.title: Use standard circle form
steps.content: A circle in standard form is $(x-h)^2+(y-k)^2=r^2$, where $(h,k)$ is the center and $r$ is the radius. Since $(x+5a)^2=(x-(-5a))^2$, circle $M$ has center $(-5a,34a)$ and radius $5a$.

steps.title: Shift the center right
steps.content:
Moving a circle $10a$ units to the right adds $10a$ to the x-coordinate of the center and leaves the y-coordinate and radius unchanged:
$$(-5a+10a,34a)=(5a,34a)$$

steps.title: Write the shifted equation
steps.content:
A circle with center $(5a,34a)$ and radius $5a$ has equation:
$$(x-5a)^2+(y-34a)^2=25a^2$$
That matches D.
